gloss: Abstraction and inference arise directly from visual learning.
Canvas patches with many color combinations reduce overfitting and learn spatial priors.
Canvas accommodates augmentations, introducing translation and scale invariance in vision.
Data augmentations encourage learning mappings invariant to geometric transformations.
Patchification incorporates inductive biases: locality and translation invariance.
Multi-view predictions are consolidated by majority voting for top-2 solutions.
Predictions from multiple canvas pixels are aggregated by average pooling.
Auxiliary tasks are translation and scale invariant.
Common sense about the visual world can be learned from training data.
Multiple unseen tasks cannot be assumed available simultaneously.
Attention masks encourage focus on foreground pixels, improving accuracy.
Model interprets ambiguous rules by proposing multiple plausible solutions.
Test-time training refines predictions by improving color and spatial arrangement.
